expectations.evidence: Word count: 81 words. Output: 'I'm Sunny — Staff Engineer at Verifiable, where I lead Developer Experience and own the automation infrastructure at the heart of the product. Before I wrote code professionally, I was three years into medical school at the Cleveland Clinic Lerner College of Medicine before I decided engineering was a better fit. I also run a consulting practice called Imagile, and in a past life I recorded an album with a thrash metal band, which I think about more than is probably healthy.' Exceeds the 75-word limit by 6 words.
Output contains: 'three years into medical school' (matches 'medical school') and 'a thrash metal band' (matches 'metal band').
'which I think about more than is probably healthy' — self-deprecating and wry. Understated rather than self-promotional. Passes the criterion.

expectations.text: Ends with the canonical meetup closer about building and tough problems
expectations.evidence: Output ends with: '...which I think about more than is probably healthy.' No occurrence of 'build great things' or 'tough business problems'. Does not contain the canonical closer.

claims.evidence: The output contains exactly 3 sentences, consistent with the prompt specification.

claims.claim: The output is suitable as a meetup intro
claims.evidence: No call-to-action or statement of what Sunny is there to do — it ends on a self-referential note about the metal band. A meetup intro should signal what kind of conversations the speaker wants to have. This one doesn't.

suggestions.assertion: Ends with the canonical meetup closer about building and tough problems
suggestions.reason: This is the most discriminating assertion in the eval and correctly fails the without_skill output. It tests a specific, skill-derived convention. The assertion is strong.
No assertion checks the 3-sentence constraint. Both outputs happen to be 3 sentences, so it didn't matter here — but it would matter if a future run produced a longer output.

eval_feedback.overall: The canonical_closer assertion is doing strong discrimination work — it's the clearest signal that the skill was applied. The word_count failure is meaningful and correctly caught. Evals are well-targeted for this format.
